title: Indonesia to Share Data Needed for Trump's Unfair Trade Probes
body: Indonesia says it will "follow the process" as the Trump administration launches two probes on unfair trade practices.

title: Indonesia Has ‘Proven Recipe’ to Handle $100 Oil, Finance Minister Says
body: "Analysts on TikTok or YouTube claiming our economy will collapse are not looking at the data at all,” he said.

title: Prabowo Calls Critics of Indonesia’s Economy ‘Unpatriotic’
body: Prabowo rebukes economists predicting gloom for Indonesia, accusing them of spreading fear and being paid to undermine the government.

title: Prabowo Targets 100 GW Solar Power Buildout in Two Years
body: Indonesia plans to build 100GW of solar power within two years as President Prabowo accelerates renewable energy and energy security efforts

title: Minister Warns Boycotts of US, Israel-Linked Products Hurt Local Workers
body: Religious affairs minister warns boycotts of US and Israel-linked products risk costing local jobs and will not resolve Middle East conflict

title: Indonesia Sees Fertilizer Export Opportunity Amid Hormuz Tension
body: Indonesia is seeing rising global demand for urea fertilizer as tensions around the Strait of Hormuz disrupt supplies from major Middle East

title: Rupiah Nears Rp 17,000 as Oil Shock and Iran Tensions Lift Dollar
body: Rupiah weakens to Rp 16,960 as oil prices surge after the Strait of Hormuz closure, while markets reassess Fed rate cut prospects.

title: JCI Sinks 3.05% as Oil Breaks $100, Iran Tensions Rattle Global Markets
body: JCI dropped 3.05% to 7,137 as oil surged above $100 amid Iran tensions, fueling global inflation fears.

title: Following Philippines, Indonesia Eyes 4-Day Workweek to Cut Fuel Use
body: Indonesia is studying WFH and a four-day workweek to cut fuel use as Middle East tensions threaten global oil supply stability.

title: Indonesia Weighs Deficit Above 3% as Oil Risks Mount, Minister Purbaya Says
body: Finance Minister Purbaya says the government is still assessing whether Indonesia’s budget deficit could widen beyond the 3% legal limit.

title: US Hits Indonesia with Another Probe, This Time on Forced Labor
body: Indonesia is also facing a separate US probe on production surpluses.

title: Regional Transfers Reach Rp147.7T by February, Economists Warn of Year-End Spending Rush
body: Indonesia has disbursed Rp147.7T in regional transfers by February, but economists warn slow spending may push budget use to year-end.

title: Antam Gold Falls to Rp 3.02M per Gram as Global Prices Slide on Stronger Dollar
body: Antam gold fell to Rp 3.02M per gram on Friday as global bullion dropped over 1% amid a stronger US dollar and fading Fed rate-cut hopes.

title: Oil Rally Toward $100 Pressures JCI, Down 0.32% at Friday Open
body: JCI fell 0.32% at Friday’s open as oil prices near $100 and escalating Gulf tensions fueled global volatility ahead of the Eid holiday.

title: Indonesia to Pursue ‘Intensive' Talks with US After Unfair Trade Probe
body: Washington has launched a probe into Indonesia to determine whether Indonesia's production surpluses have jeopardized American production.

title: Ramadan Exodus to Generate Rp 190T in Seasonal Economic Activity
body: The seasonal migration, known locally as mudik, has become a key driver of short-term economic activity.

title: Government’s Playbook for Expensive Oil: Biofuels, Strategic Reserves, and Budget Discipline
body: Finance Ministry simulations suggest the fiscal deficit could widen to 3.6% of GDP if oil prices average $92 per barrel this year.

title: Explainer: US to Release 172 Million Barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
body: The International Energy Agency pledged to release 400 million barrels of oil available from its member nations’ stockpiles.

title: Indonesia to Phase Out Diesel Power Plants as Solar Projects Begin
body: Indonesia plans to phase out diesel power plants as solar and geothermal projects expand to boost clean energy and electrify remote areas.
